goal: wave12-v2-objects: Ship SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 12 (Phase 2 object store + visibility) per docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md. Build the unified knowledge/community object store (generalizing the existing contributions table where sensible), enforce the §5.1 visibility model (public / members-only / private) at query time for both human sessions and agent bearer tokens, generalize the story moderation queue into generic contribution draft/pending states with member publish settings (auto-publish vs review) and an auto-moderation hook, add a derived-article workflow stub, and ship the personal layer (bookmarks, notes, annotations, comments split, recently-viewed) with a persisted onboarding profile — replacing the Wave 11 localStorage stubs with real backend APIs. Wire the .io My Library (bookmarks/notes/recently-viewed) and move the assessment under /insights (assessment-as-Insight UX). Wire the .org Knowledge/Community object rendering with visibility, My Library / My articles / My comments, the generic moderation/publish UI, and persist the Ask Submit/Capture/Find-Help drafts via the new object/contribution endpoints. Phase 1 IA shell (Wave 11) is already merged to main; this wave is the backend object model + visibility enforcement + personal layer + their frontend wiring. Community Phase 1 type write-API full parity is Wave 13; keep community write paths as draft/stub where §11 reserves them. Code and docs stay in English (project-guidelines).
summary: Jacky's bot: shipping SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 12 — Phase 2 object store, visibility enforcement, generic moderation/publish, and the personal layer (My Library) on .io and .org
acceptanceCriteria: Repo builds (pnpm turbo build) and backend tests pass on the integrated branch
Backend exposes a unified knowledge/community object store with a visibility column (public | members-only | private) and per-site scoping

Visibility is ENFORCED at query time: anonymous sees only public; a logged-in member of a site sees public + that site's members-only; private is owner-only (owner session or owner's agent bearer token). No members-only/private leak to anonymous or cross-user/cross-site requests
Story moderation queue generalized to generic contribution draft/pending/published states with member auto-publish vs review-before-publish setting and an auto-moderation hook on submit

Personal layer persisted in the backend (bookmarks, private notes incl. Capture output, annotations, public comments, recently-viewed) + persisted onboarding profile, with typed shared Zod schemas + API client methods replacing the Wave 11 localStorage stubs
.io My Library wired to bookmarks/notes/recently-viewed APIs; assessment surfaced under /insights
.org Knowledge/Community render objects with visibility; My Library / My articles / My comments wired; generic moderation/publish UI; Ask Capture/Submit/Find Help persist via new endpoints
Shared contracts (@ai-transformation/shared) define object, visibility, moderation/publish, and personal-layer Zod schemas + createApiClient methods used by BOTH backend and frontends
One integrated draft PR to main

tasks.name: shared-foundation
tasks.scopedGoal: Define the Wave 12 shared contract that the backend implements and BOTH frontends consume, in packages/shared ONLY. This is the single source of truth for the object store, visibility, moderation/publish, and personal layer; backend and frontend workers run in parallel against it, so it must be complete, typed, and stable. Deliver, as new files re-exported from packages/shared/src/index.ts: (1) OBJECT MODEL — a `Visibility` union ('public' | 'members-only' | 'private') per SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §5.1; a `KnowledgeObjectType` ('article' | 'field_note' | 'derived_article') and `CommunityObjectType` ('discussion' | 'help_request' | 'event' | 'community_announcement') per §5.2/§5.3 (Phase 2 community types may be a reserved string-extensible enum); a generic object record schema with id, objectType (knowledge|community discriminant), type (the specific type above), site ('io'|'org'), ownerUserId (nullable), visibility, title/subject, body, status, metadata (record), sourceContributionId (nullable, links back to contributions), createdAt/updatedAt, publishedSlug (nullable); request/response schemas for list (with visibility-aware filters: site, objectType, type, mine) and get-by-id. (2) MODERATION & PUBLISH — a generic contribution/object lifecycle status union (e.g. 'draft' | 'pending' | 'published' | 'featured' | 'archived' | 'rejected') that supersedes the story-specific statuses while staying compatible with the existing `contributions.status` values; a per-user publish-preference schema ({ defaultPublishMode: 'auto' | 'review' }); an auto-moderation result schema ({ allowed: boolean, reasons?: string[] }) used by the submit hook. (3) PERSONAL LAYER — schemas for bookmark, note (private; Capture output is a note with a capture flag/source), annotation (private), comment (public, attaches to a target by { targetType, targetId } where targetType covers 'library_article' filesystem slug AND 'object' DB id), recently-viewed entry; each carries visibility per §5.4 (bookmarks/notes/annotations/recently-viewed = private, comments = public). (4) PERSISTED ONBOARDING PROFILE — extend/wrap the existing onboardingProfileSchema (role, industry, optional projectFocus) with a persisted profile record (userId, profile, publishPreference) so it can move off localStorage. (5) API CLIENT — extend `createApiClient(baseUrl)` with typed methods for every new endpoint the backend will expose: objects.list/get, contributions/objects create+draft+submit, moderation queue list + transition, publish-preference get/set, profile get/set, bookmarks/notes/annotations/comments/recently-viewed CRUD+list. Use clear REST-ish paths under /api/v1 for agent-parity reads/writes and /api for session endpoints, matching the existing lane conventions you can see in the repo. Do NOT implement server logic — only the schemas + client method signatures (the contract). Keep everything generic and minimal; do not over-engineer. Run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck` (or tsc --noEmit in the package) and ensure it passes. Do NOT touch apps/**, packages/content/**, data/**, docs/**.
tasks.brief: READ FIRST: docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §4 (Ask modes + unified API note), §5 (object model: 5.1 visibility, 5.2 knowledge objects, 5.3 community objects + Phase 1 vs Phase 2 types, 5.4 personal layer), §7 (moderation & publish pipeline), §9 (recommendations inputs), §11 Phase 2. Also read packages/shared/src/index.ts (existing schemas: contributionSourceSchema, storyStatusSchema, moderation schemas, auth, assessment, createApiClient) and packages/shared/src/{onboarding,ask-modes,recommendation}.ts (Wave 11 additions you must build on, not duplicate). This contract is consumed in PARALLEL by the backend worker and both frontend workers — they branch from you, so missing or wrong schemas cause silent drift across three workers. Make the object record generic enough to cover BOTH knowledge and community objects with one table on the backend, and make visibility a first-class field. The comment target is polymorphic so comments can attach to filesystem library articles (by slug) AND DB objects (by id). Keep the existing contributions schemas working; ADD the generic lifecycle status as a superset rather than breaking story statuses. English code per project-guidelines. Phase 2 only: no Wave 13 full community write parity — but the schemas should not block it.
acceptance: @ai-transformation/shared exports a Visibility union and generic knowledge+community object record schema (objectType, type, site, ownerUserId, visibility, status, sourceContributionId, metadata) + list/get request+response schemas with visibility-aware filters
@ai-transformation/shared exports a generic contribution/object lifecycle status union, a per-user publish-preference schema, and an auto-moderation result schema
@ai-transformation/shared exports personal-layer schemas (bookmark, note incl. capture flag, annotation, public comment with polymorphic target, recently-viewed) with §5.4 visibility defaults
@ai-transformation/shared exports a persisted onboarding-profile record wrapping the existing onboardingProfileSchema plus publishPreference
createApiClient gains typed methods for objects list/get, create/draft/submit, moderation list+transition, publish-preference get/set, profile get/set, and personal-layer CRUD/list
Existing shared schemas (contributions, story statuses, ask-modes, onboarding, recommendation) remain exported and unbroken; packages/shared typecheck passes

tasks.name: backend-objects
tasks.scopedGoal: Implement the Wave 12 backend object store + visibility enforcement + generic moderation/publish in apps/backend ONLY, consuming the @ai-transformation/shared contract from the shared-foundation branch you are based on. The backend is Hono + better-sqlite3 with inline idempotent migrations in src/db/*.ts (runMigrations chains run*Migrations on getDb() at boot) and lanes mounted in src/index.ts; follow those conventions exactly (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS; if you must evolve an existing column, add an idempotent ALTER guarded by a pragma/table_info check). Deliver: (1) OBJECT STORE — a new `objects` table (or generalize as the shared contract dictates) with columns matching the shared object record: id, object_type (knowledge|community), type, site, owner_user_id (nullable), visibility ('public'|'members-only'|'private'), title/subject, body, status, metadata JSON, source_contribution_id (nullable FK to contributions), published_slug, created_at, updated_at; with indexes on (site, object_type, visibility, status) and owner_user_id. Add a db/objects.ts module with typed CRUD + a list query that takes the requester context. (2) VISIBILITY ENFORCEMENT — a single authoritative query-time filter used by every read path: anonymous => visibility='public' only; logged-in member of site S => public OR (members-only AND site=S); private => only when requesterUserId === owner_user_id OR the agent bearer token's resolved owner email maps to that owner. Per-site members-only: a member of .io must NOT see .org members-only objects and vice versa (site is derived from x-forwarded-host like the existing session/site logic). NEVER leak members-only/private rows to anonymous or to a different user/site. (3) ENDPOINTS — mount a lane (e.g. src/lanes/objects/index.ts) exposing the shared client's object endpoints: GET list (session-aware AND bearer-aware, applying the visibility filter), GET by id (404 or 403 semantics that don't leak existence beyond intent), POST create/draft, POST submit (runs the auto-moderation hook below), under both the session path (/api) and the agent path (/api/v1) per the shared contract so on-site Ask and external agents share contracts (§4 unified-API note). (4) MODERATION & PUBLISH — generalize the story moderation queue: keep the existing /api/stories endpoints working (web-org/moderation depends on them) but back them by the generic lifecycle states; add a generic moderation queue endpoint (list pending/draft across sources/objects + a transition endpoint draft|pending->published|featured|archived|rejected) gated by the existing ADMIN_EMAILS/isAdmin gate; add per-user publish preference persistence (table or column) with get/set endpoints; implement the auto-moderation hook (a pure, deterministic content-policy stub returning the shared auto-moderation result — e.g. simple banned-pattern/length checks — wired so submit with publish preference 'auto' AND allowed => published within visibility rules, never elevating members-only/private to public; 'review' => pending). (5) DERIVED-ARTICLE WORKFLOW STUB — an endpoint/helper that creates a knowledge derived_article object from a community discussion object id and writes a back-link in the discussion's metadata per §5.2 derivation rule (stub-level: create the object + link; no LLM). (6) TESTS — add backend tests (the repo uses the backend test runner; mirror existing src tests) that prove the visibility matrix: anonymous, member-of-io, member-of-org, owner, and bearer-owner each see exactly the rows they should and NOTHING they shouldn't, plus auto-moderation auto-publish vs review, plus the existing stories/moderation endpoints still pass. Run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` (and typecheck) and ensure green. Do NOT touch packages/**, apps/web-io/**, apps/web-org/**, apps/combined/**, data/**, docs/**.
tasks.brief: READ FIRST: docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §5 (visibility + object model), §7 (moderation/publish). Then read apps/backend/src/db/index.ts (contributions/users/sessions tables, runMigrations, insertContribution + story helpers), apps/backend/src/db/chat.ts and agent-protocol.ts (module migration pattern), apps/backend/src/index.ts (lane mounting + sessionMiddleware on /api/*), apps/backend/src/middleware/session.ts (atx_session cookie -> c.get('user')), apps/backend/src/lanes/harvest/index.ts (stories/moderation endpoints + ADMIN_EMAILS gate + lib/admin.ts), and apps/backend/src/lanes/agent-protocol/index.ts (bearer write-token + /api/v1 + read quota; how it resolves the agent's owner email). LOAD .agents/skills/lane-backend-core/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/lane-harvest/SKILL.md before editing. Visibility enforcement is the security core of this wave — implement ONE filter helper and route every read through it; do not hand-roll per-endpoint filters that can drift. Do NOT migrate the filesystem knowledge-base/ or packages/content articles into SQLite — those stay public filesystem library content; the object store is for user/agent-contributed knowledge + community objects + their visibility. Keep contributions as the submission inbox and link objects to it via source_contribution_id rather than a big-bang rewrite. The frontends build against the shared API client in PARALLEL with you and rely on you honoring the shared request/response Zod shapes EXACTLY — do not invent divergent wire shapes; if a shape is missing in shared, define a backend-internal type but keep the wire contract matching shared. English code, no narrative comments (project-guidelines + karpathy-guidelines).
acceptance: New objects store (table + db/objects.ts CRUD) with object_type, type, site, owner_user_id, visibility, status, source_contribution_id, metadata; idempotent boot migration following the existing pattern

Single authoritative visibility filter applied to every read: anonymous=public only; site member=public+that-site members-only; private=owner (session) or owner-bearer only; per-site members-only isolation; no leaks
Object lane endpoints (list/get/create/draft/submit) mounted on BOTH /api (session) and /api/v1 (bearer) per the shared contract, applying the visibility filter

Story moderation queue generalized to generic lifecycle states with existing /api/stories endpoints still working; generic moderation-queue list + transition endpoint behind ADMIN_EMAILS gate; per-user publish preference get/set; auto-moderation hook wired (auto-publish vs review; never elevates members-only/private to public)
Derived-article workflow stub: create a knowledge derived_article from a discussion id and write a back-link in the discussion metadata

Backend tests prove the full visibility matrix (anonymous/io-member/org-member/owner/bearer-owner) and auto-moderation auto vs review; existing stories/moderation tests still pass; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and backend typecheck green
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- From repo root on this branch: `pnpm install` if needed.
## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` must pass, including new visibility-matrix and auto-moderation tests.
- Backend typecheck must pass.
## Manual
- Boot the backend; confirm migrations create the objects table; hit the object list endpoint anonymously (public only), then with a seeded io member session (adds io members-only), then org member (adds org members-only, NOT io's), then owner (adds own private). Confirm a bearer token resolves owner private access only for its owner.
- Confirm /api/stories + /api/stories/moderation still behave; confirm submit with publish pref auto+allowed publishes (within visibility) and review goes pending.
## Gotchas
- One visibility filter, every read path. Per-site members-only isolation. Never elevate members-only/private to public. Do not migrate filesystem articles into SQLite.

tasks.name: backend-personal
tasks.scopedGoal: Implement the Wave 12 personal layer + persisted onboarding profile in apps/backend ONLY, building on the backend-objects branch you are based on (it already added the objects store, visibility filter, and generic moderation/publish). Consume the @ai-transformation/shared personal-layer + profile contract. Deliver, following the same Hono + better-sqlite3 inline-migration conventions (add a db/personal.ts module with runPersonalMigrations chained into runMigrations; CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + indexes): (1) PERSONAL TABLES — bookmarks (user_id, target_type, target_id, created_at), notes (user_id, body, is_capture flag/source, visibility default private, created_at/updated_at), annotations (user_id, target_type, target_id, body, anchor metadata, private), comments (user_id, target_type, target_id, body, PUBLIC visibility, created_at) where target_type covers 'library_article' (filesystem slug) and 'object' (DB object id), recently_viewed (user_id, target_type, target_id, viewed_at; upsert most-recent). (2) PROFILE — a user_profiles table (user_id PK, profile JSON: role/industry/projectFocus, publish_preference) with get/set, replacing the Wave 11 localStorage stub as the server source of truth. (3) ENDPOINTS — session-authenticated /api routes for each: bookmarks list/create/delete, notes list/create/update/delete (Capture writes a note with the capture flag; always private), annotations list/create/delete, comments list (public, by target)/create/delete-own, recently-viewed list/upsert, profile get/set, exactly matching the shared createApiClient method shapes. Private items (bookmarks/notes/annotations/recently-viewed) are owner-only reads; public comments are world-readable by target but only the author may delete their own. Respect and reuse the visibility helper from backend-objects where comments/notes interact with object visibility. (4) TESTS — backend tests covering: a user only reads their own private personal rows; another user cannot read or delete them; public comments are readable by anyone for a target but only the author can delete; profile get/set round-trips; Capture creates a private note. Keep all existing backend tests (including backend-objects' visibility tests) passing. Run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` + backend typecheck; ensure green. Do NOT touch packages/**, apps/web-io/**, apps/web-org/**, apps/combined/**, data/**, docs/**.
tasks.brief: You start from the backend-objects branch — read its handoff (pasted into your prompt) for the exact table/module/endpoint conventions and the visibility helper location so you reuse rather than duplicate it. READ docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §5.4 (personal layer table: bookmarks/comments/annotations/notes/recently-viewed/my-articles/my-comments + their visibility) and §8 (onboarding profile fields feed recommendations). Read apps/backend/src/db/index.ts + the new db/objects.ts to mirror the module/migration pattern, and apps/backend/src/middleware/session.ts for how to get the current user. LOAD .agents/skills/lane-backend-core/SKILL.md. The frontends consume these via the shared API client and will replace their Wave 11 localStorage stubs (io.recently-viewed.v1, io.capture-notes.v1, io.onboarding.profile.v1, org LocalDraftStub). Match the shared client shapes EXACTLY. Private personal data must be strictly owner-scoped — write tests that prove cross-user isolation. English code, no narrative comments.
acceptance: db/personal.ts adds bookmarks, notes (with capture flag), annotations, comments, recently_viewed, and user_profiles tables via idempotent boot migration chained into runMigrations
Session-authenticated endpoints for bookmarks, notes, annotations, comments, recently-viewed, and profile get/set matching the shared createApiClient shapes

Private rows (bookmarks/notes/annotations/recently-viewed) are strictly owner-only; public comments readable by target but author-only delete; comment/note interactions reuse the backend-objects visibility helper
Profile get/set persists role/industry/projectFocus + publishPreference as the server source of truth (replacing localStorage)

Backend tests prove cross-user isolation of private personal data, public-comment read/author-delete semantics, and profile round-trip; all prior backend tests still pass; backend test + typecheck green
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `pnpm install` if needed on this branch.
## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` must pass, including new personal-layer isolation tests AND the inherited backend-objects visibility tests.
## Manual
- With two seeded user sessions, confirm user A cannot read/delete user B's bookmarks/notes/annotations/recently-viewed; confirm public comments on a target are visible to both but only the author deletes; confirm profile get/set round-trips.
## Gotchas
- Reuse the backend-objects visibility helper; don't fork it. Private personal data is owner-only. Capture = private note.

tasks.name: web-io-personal
tasks.scopedGoal: Wire the ai-transformation.io Wave 12 personal layer + assessment-under-Insights in apps/web-io (+ data/curated/io-*.json), consuming the new @ai-transformation/shared object/personal/profile schemas and createApiClient methods from the shared-foundation branch you are based on. The backend that implements these endpoints is built in PARALLEL on a sibling branch and is NOT reachable from your clone at build time — so build against the shared API client, keep all fetches SSR-safe and resilient to the backend being unavailable (graceful loading/empty/error states), and DO NOT hard-fail the build on a missing backend. Deliver: (1) MY LIBRARY — replace the Wave 11 localStorage stubs (lib/recently-viewed.ts, lib/captured-notes.ts, lib/onboarding-profile.ts and the library-browser MyLibrary placeholder) so bookmarks, private notes (incl. Capture output), and recently-viewed are read/written through the shared API client when the user is logged in, with a localStorage fallback only for logged-out/offline. The My Library tab should list real bookmarks + notes + recently-viewed from the API for a signed-in user. (2) CAPTURE — Ask mode=capture writes a private note via the notes endpoint (still private per §4) instead of pure localStorage when signed in. (3) ONBOARDING PROFILE — persist role/industry/projectFocus through the profile get/set API for signed-in users (Settings + first-login), falling back to localStorage when logged out; feed the persisted profile into the existing recommendation scorer inputs. (4) ASSESSMENT UNDER INSIGHTS — surface the assessment as an Insight data type per §10: add /insights/assessment (or an Insights-hub entry) that launches the existing assessment wizard, ensure assessment is reachable under Insights (not as a standalone ribbon item — Wave 11 already removed the standalone hub), and feed completed assessment results into the recommendation scorer's weakest-gap boost on the logged-in Home dashboard (§9 input 2). (5) BOOKMARK / SAVE-TO-CONTEXT actions on Library + Insights items call the bookmarks API for signed-in users; the contextual Open-in-Ask prefills from Wave 11 stay working. (6) Keep ribbon labels and routes from Wave 11 unchanged (Home·Library·Insights·Ask); this wave adds persistence + assessment-under-Insights, not new nav. Update data/curated/io-home.json / io-hub-index.json only if a CTA/path needs to point at /insights/assessment. Ensure web-io builds (`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` or the repo's web-io build). Do NOT touch apps/web-org/**, packages/**, apps/backend/**, apps/combined/**.
tasks.brief: READ FIRST: docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §2 (.io cockpit), §3.3 io routes, §5.4 personal layer, §9 recommendations inputs, §10 Insights (assessment is a data type under Insights), §11 Phase 2. LOAD .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md before UI work (override generic frontend skills; content-first editorial, light default + dark toggle, English-only UI). Read the Wave 11 web-io stubs you are replacing: lib/recently-viewed.ts, lib/captured-notes.ts, lib/onboarding-profile.ts, lib/recommendations.ts, components library-browser.tsx (MyLibrary), the Ask capture mode, and the logged-in Home dashboard; auth state comes from components/auth-nav.tsx (client fetch /api/auth/me). The shared-foundation branch you start from defines the object/personal/profile Zod schemas + createApiClient methods — import and use them; do not invent your own fetch shapes (the backend implements the same contract). Backend is built in parallel and unreachable at build time: degrade gracefully, never block the build, and keep a localStorage fallback for logged-out. Capture stays PRIVATE. If a UI interaction changed, capture a short screen recording or before/after screenshots and reference the artifact path in your handoff.
acceptance: My Library reads/writes bookmarks, private notes (incl. Capture), and recently-viewed via the shared API client for signed-in users, with localStorage fallback for logged-out; MyLibrary tab lists real API data when signed in
Ask mode=capture persists a private note via the notes endpoint when signed in
Onboarding profile (role/industry/projectFocus) persists via the profile API for signed-in users and feeds the recommendation scorer; localStorage fallback when logged out
Assessment is surfaced under /insights (e.g. /insights/assessment) using the existing wizard; completed results feed the weakest-gap recommendation boost on the logged-in Home dashboard
Bookmark/Save-to-context on Library + Insights items calls the bookmarks API for signed-in users; Wave 11 Open-in-Ask prefills still work
Ribbon labels/routes unchanged from Wave 11; web-io builds (SSR-safe; no build failure when backend is unreachable)
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Build web-io with the repo build (e.g. `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build`).
## Automated
- Build + web-io typecheck must pass even with no backend reachable.
## Manual
- Run web-io dev; signed-out: My Library uses localStorage fallback, assessment reachable under /insights. Signed-in (or mocked): My Library shows bookmarks/notes/recently-viewed from the API client; Capture saves a private note; profile persists; assessment completion boosts recommendations on Home.
## Gotchas
- Backend built in parallel, unreachable at build: degrade gracefully, never block build. Capture stays private. No new ribbon items — assessment lives under Insights.

tasks.name: web-org-personal
tasks.scopedGoal: Wire the ai-transformation.org Wave 12 object rendering + personal layer + generic moderation/publish UI in apps/web-org (+ data/curated/org-*.json), consuming the new @ai-transformation/shared object/personal/moderation/profile schemas and createApiClient methods from the shared-foundation branch you are based on. The backend implementing these endpoints is built in PARALLEL on a sibling branch and is NOT reachable from your clone at build time — build against the shared API client, keep all fetches SSR-safe and resilient (graceful loading/empty/error states), and never hard-fail the build when the backend is unavailable. Deliver: (1) KNOWLEDGE renders objects with VISIBILITY: /knowledge lists knowledge objects via the shared client's visibility-aware list (anonymous sees public; signed-in members additionally see members-only); individual /knowledge/<slug or id> renders an object; logged-in adds My Library / My articles / My comments tabs backed by the personal + objects APIs (replace the Wave 11 placeholder tabs). Filesystem library articles remain rendered as before; the object list augments them — do not delete the existing article rendering. (2) COMMUNITY renders objects per §5.3 with visibility: /community public-only highlights for anonymous (discussion, help_request, event, community_announcement) and the fuller opportunity layer (all visibility-allowed items) when signed in, fetched via the shared client; keep action verbs (Reply, Save/Bookmark, Follow placeholder, Draft via Ask) — wire Save/Bookmark to the bookmarks API; full write/reply parity stays Wave 13 (keep as draft/stub where the contract reserves it). (3) ASK MODES persist: Capture => private note via notes API; Submit => a contribution/object DRAFT via the create/draft endpoint (member review or auto-publish per the user's publish preference, honoring visibility — never auto-elevate to public); Find Help => a help_request community object draft via the create endpoint. Replace the Wave 11 org LocalDraftStub (localStorage atx-org-ask-*) with real API writes when signed in, localStorage fallback when logged out. (4) MODERATION/PUBLISH UI: generalize the existing /moderation page (currently stories-only via /api/stories/moderation) to the generic moderation queue endpoint (list draft/pending across sources/objects + transition publish/feature/archive/reject) from the shared client, keeping admin-gating; add the per-user publish-preference setting (auto-publish vs review-before-publish) in Settings (replace the Wave 11 'coming with publish pipeline' stub) via the profile/publish-preference API. (5) PERSONAL profile persists via the profile API (same as io). (6) Keep ribbon labels/routes from Wave 11 (Home·Knowledge·Community·Ask) and the 'Community · Knowledge commons' brand unchanged; this wave adds object rendering + persistence, not new nav. Update data/curated/org-home.json / org-hub-index.json only if a CTA/path must change. Ensure web-org builds. Do NOT touch apps/web-io/**, packages/**, apps/backend/**, apps/combined/**.
tasks.brief: READ FIRST: docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §2 (.org positioning), §3.3 org routes, §5 (object model + visibility + 5.3 community types + 5.4 personal layer), §6 contextual actions, §7 moderation/publish, §11 Phase 2. LOAD .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md before UI work (override generic frontend skills; English-only UI, light default + dark toggle). Read the Wave 11 web-org surfaces you are extending/replacing: components knowledge-index-view.tsx (hardcoded 'public' label + placeholder My Library/My articles tabs), ask-modes.tsx (LocalDraftStub localStorage atx-org-ask-*), onboarding-profile-form.tsx, community-activity-dashboard.tsx, settings/page.tsx (publish stub), app/moderation (ModerationPanel + /api/stories/moderation). If a UI interaction changed, capture a short screen recording / before-after screenshots and reference the artifact path in your handoff.
acceptance: /knowledge lists knowledge objects via the visibility-aware shared client (public for anonymous, +members-only for signed-in members) alongside existing filesystem articles; /knowledge/<id|slug> renders an object; logged-in My Library/My articles/My comments tabs backed by personal+objects APIs (placeholders replaced)
/community renders objects per §5.3 with visibility (public-only for anonymous, fuller layer signed-in); Save/Bookmark wired to bookmarks API; full reply/write parity left as Wave 13 draft/stub

Ask Capture => private note API; Submit => contribution/object draft via create/draft (auto vs review per publish preference, never auto-elevates to public); Find Help => help_request draft; org LocalDraftStub replaced with API writes when signed in (localStorage fallback logged-out)

/moderation generalized to the generic moderation queue (list + transition) from the shared client, admin-gated; Settings exposes per-user auto-publish vs review preference via the publish-preference API (Wave 11 stub replaced)
Onboarding profile persists via the profile API; ribbon labels/routes + 'Community · Knowledge commons' brand unchanged from Wave 11
web-org builds (SSR-safe; no build failure when backend is unreachable)

tasks.name: integrate-wave12
tasks.scopedGoal: Produce the single integrated Wave 12 deliverable branch and open ONE draft PR to main. You start from the backend-personal branch (which already contains backend-objects and shared-foundation as ancestors — the full backend + shared contract). Merge `orch/wave12-v2-objects/web-io-personal` and `orch/wave12-v2-objects/web-org-personal` into the current branch (both share shared-foundation as common ancestor; their app/data paths are disjoint from the backend and from each other, so conflicts should be minimal — if any arise in shared/content/docs, keep BOTH sites' and the backend's consumption working; never drop a site's or the backend's needs). After merging: run `pnpm install` then `pnpm turbo build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` from repo root; fix integration-only breakage (import drift, type mismatches where a frontend's use of a shared client method diverged from the backend's implementation — reconcile at the shared-contract level so both compile and the wire shapes agree). Confirm the frontends consume the SAME shared client methods the backend implements (no shadow fetch shapes). Then update docs to reflect Wave 12 shipped: docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md and docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md (note Phase 2 object store + visibility enforcement live, generic moderation/publish + publish preferences, personal layer / My Library on both sites, assessment-under-Insights on .io; Phase 3 / Wave 13 = community Phase 1 types full write API + Ask Submit/Find Help parity with external agent). Commit, push exactly your branch, and open a DRAFT PR to main titled 'Wave 12: SITE_DESIGN_v2 Phase 2 object store + visibility + personal layer' summarizing the backend object/visibility/moderation work and both sites' wiring. Do NOT merge the PR or mark it ready.
tasks.brief: This is a merge + integration + docs task (planners do not code, so this worker performs the merge). Branch topology: shared-foundation -> backend-objects -> backend-personal (your start); web-io-personal and web-org-personal both branch from shared-foundation. Merging the two frontends into backend-personal joins at the shared-foundation common ancestor; backend files and each app's files are disjoint, so the main real risk is divergent use of shared client methods (a frontend calling a shape the backend didn't implement, or vice versa) — reconcile those at the type/contract level so `pnpm turbo build` passes for web-io AND web-org and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` stays green. Read the four upstream handoffs (pasted into your prompt) for each branch's actual name and any deviations. Keep merge history with both parents. English docs/code per project-guidelines. After build+test green, update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md, then open the draft PR via ManagePullRequest against main.
acceptance: web-io-personal and web-org-personal merged into the backend-personal-based deliverable branch with both parents in history; full backend + shared + both frontends present
pnpm turbo build passes for web-io and web-org
pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test passes (visibility matrix + personal-layer isolation + existing suites)
Frontends and backend agree on the shared client contract (no divergent wire shapes); integration type/import drift resolved
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to reflect Wave 12 shipped and Wave 13 next
One DRAFT PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready) summarizing object store + visibility + moderation/publish + personal layer on both sites
tasks.verify:

## Automated

## Manual

## Gotchas

tasks.name: verify-wave12
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the integrated Wave 12 branch against the planner acceptance criteria and SITE_DESIGN_v2.md Phase 2. Build the repo (`pnpm turbo build`) and run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`. The SECURITY-CRITICAL focus is visibility enforcement: confirm via the backend tests AND, where feasible, by booting the backend and exercising the object list/get endpoints that (a) anonymous requests return ONLY public objects, (b) a logged-in member of a site additionally sees that site's members-only objects but NOT the other site's members-only, (c) private objects are returned only to their owner (owner session or owner's agent bearer token) and to no one else, and (d) personal-layer private rows (bookmarks/notes/annotations/recently-viewed) are strictly owner-scoped while public comments are world-readable by target but author-only deletable. Also confirm: the object store table + visibility filter exist and every read path routes through the single filter; the story moderation queue is generalized to generic lifecycle states with the existing /api/stories endpoints still working; per-user auto-publish vs review preference exists and auto-publish never elevates members-only/private to public; the derived-article stub creates a knowledge object + discussion back-link; the .io My Library + Capture + profile + assessment-under-Insights wiring is present; the .org Knowledge/Community object rendering with visibility, My Library/My articles/My comments, generic /moderation UI, publish-preference Settings, and Ask Capture/Submit/Find Help API persistence are present; both sites build. Report execution evidence per criterion — do NOT pattern-match. If the env blocks a live UI/backend check, run the build + backend test suite + code/route existence checks and mark unit-test-verified or verifier-blocked honestly; do not round up.
acceptance: Verification section includes execution evidence (build output, backend test results incl. the visibility matrix + personal-layer isolation tests, and route/code existence checks) for every integrate-wave12 acceptance criterion and the planner-level visibility-enforcement and moderation/publish requirements
